title: Fix: Multiple Accept Headers
body:
<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->

There can be multiple Accept Headers those can be added as a comma separated values.

Fixes: https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/issues/9902

comments.body:
And of course we want an answer to this before we can consider this:

> If the proxy behaves contrary to the RFC, why shouldn’t this be fixed at the proxy?

> If the proxy behaves contrary to the RFC, why shouldn’t this be fixed at the proxy?

I’m not _too_ dogmatic about this; containerd and go-containerregistry do produce comma-separated headers, so there is a good argument that changing c/image would not break anything, and if it helped, well, fine.

But, yeah, changing c/image is a very clear _second_ best approach:
- Even in the best possible case, changing c/image will take weeks/months to get to production; changing a cloud proxy might be much faster
- It _seems_ unlikely that this interoperability problem was never reported before. Isn’t there more to the story?
- If the proxy doesn’t accept valid HTTP, there’s a high likelihood there are _other_ kinds of valid HTTP it doesn’t accept; so there should be a pretty good understanding of what is going on or why before committing to such a workaround (with this long lead time)

Thanks everyone for the thorough review and discussion!

After looking at the feedback, the general consensus in the containers/CRI-O community seems to be that since the current behavior is spec-compliant and nothing is functionally broken, it's not worth making this change — especially given the limited impact of the issue.

Closing this out. I'll keep an eye on the upstream proxy/CRI-O side in case the situation changes.

(Side note: I'm relatively new to the containers and Kubernetes ecosystem and have been using PRs like this to get my hands dirty — appreciate the patience and detailed explanations from the reviewers!)
